title: Charmed Life - Chapter 20 (Fragment 2)
text:
One of the most challenging tasks a Chrestomanci has to undertake is finding his successor — the next Chrestomanci. This is never easy, not only because of the fact that nine-lifed enchanters are exceedingly rare, but they might not even be living in the same world! They also are usually completely unaware that they have more than the usual number of lives and often demonstrate a distinct lack of magical ability in childhood. They are often discovered for the first time when they begin to lose their lives. For example, if on Monday somebody falls from an extremely high tower, or is consumed by fire, or gets hit round the head with a cricket bat, and yet on Tuesday is happily walking around with no noticeable ill-effects, chances are he has nine lives. This means that he is then worth testing to find out what’s stopping him from doing magic.

All nine-lifed enchanters have a weakness, and it is usually that weakness which prevents them from being aware of their magic when they are young. Of course, they do not all share the same weakness, so it can take some time to track down exactly what the problem is. Once the weakness is identified and avoided, they can work magic like anything! In fact, because enchanters’ magic is particularly potent and immediate, they have to spend many years in training, learning how to control their power and discovering its full extent — while trying very hard not to dislocate the universe or blow off too many rooftops.

Once they are aware of their nine lives, it’s up to them to make sure they take care of the ones they have left — especially the last one. The best way to do this is to detach it from the body and keep it in a secret, protected location where it cannot be attacked or enchanted. Removing a life intact is a delicate operation; Gabriel de Witt, who is Chrestomanci in The Lives of Christopher Chant , pioneered the process, trialling it successfully for the first time on his Chrestomanci-in-training.

The one thing Chrestomanci doesn’t have is a nine-to-five job. He h11as to be ready to thwart evil, apprehend villains, and solve magical conundrums at the drop of a hat, no matter in what time, place, or dimension they happen to occur. People in dire need can also summon him by calling his name aloud three times. Consequently, his staff are used to finding half-drunk cups of tea and abandoned plates, as dire need is no respecter of mealtimes.

Being Chrestomanci is the most powerful job in the related worlds, and the Government are always on the lookout for the next one. So if you ever find yourself returning to dream worlds you’ve visited before, waking up with grubby feet and gravel in your bed, being told off for being “on a different planet,” or raising small whirlwinds in the garden — you never know, it could be you!

vocabulary.definition: To arrest or capture (someone, especially a criminal).
vocabulary.example: He has to be ready to thwart evil, apprehend villains, and solve magical conundrums.

vocabulary.definition: A result or effect of an action or condition.
vocabulary.example: Consequently, his staff are used to finding half-drunk cups of tea.

vocabulary.definition: A confusing and difficult problem or question.
vocabulary.example: He has to solve magical conundrums at the drop of a hat.

vocabulary.definition: Very fine in texture, quality, or construction; requiring careful handling.
vocabulary.example: Removing a life intact is a delicate operation.

vocabulary.definition: To clearly show the existence or truth of (something) by giving proof or evidence.
vocabulary.example: They often demonstrate a distinct lack of magical ability in childhood.

vocabulary.definition: To separate or disengage something from something else.
vocabulary.example: The best way to do this is to detach it from the body and keep it in a secret location.

vocabulary.definition: Extremely serious or urgent.
vocabulary.example: People in dire need can also summon him...

vocabulary.definition: To disturb the normal position of (a joint or other part).
vocabulary.example: While trying very hard not to dislocate the universe or blow off too many rooftops.

vocabulary.definition: Recognizably different in nature from something else of a similar type.
vocabulary.example: They often demonstrate a distinct lack of magical ability in childhood.

vocabulary.definition: A person who uses magic or enchantments.
vocabulary.example: ...because of the fact that nine-lifed enchanters are exceedingly rare.

vocabulary.definition: To an extreme degree; very.
vocabulary.example: ...because of the fact that nine-lifed enchanters are exceedingly rare.

vocabulary.definition: Dirty or grimy, especially with sweat or dirt.
vocabulary.example: So if you ever find yourself waking up with grubby feet and gravel in your bed...

vocabulary.definition: To recognize or establish what something is.
vocabulary.example: Once the weakness is identified and avoided, they can work magic like anything!

vocabulary.definition: Occurring or done at once; instant.
vocabulary.example: Enchanters’ magic is particularly potent and immediate.

vocabulary.definition: An expression referring to a mythical attribute of having nine lives, implying extreme resilience or survival.
vocabulary.example: Chances are he has nine lives.

vocabulary.definition: To develop or be the first to use or apply (a new method, area of knowledge, or activity).
vocabulary.example: Gabriel de Witt pioneered the process, trialling it successfully for the first time.

vocabulary.definition: Having great power, influence, or effect.
vocabulary.example: Enchanters’ magic is particularly potent and immediate.

vocabulary.definition: To stop something from happening or someone from doing something.
vocabulary.example: It is usually that weakness which prevents them from being aware of their magic when they are young.

vocabulary.definition: To cause to grow or increase; to bring about.
vocabulary.example: Being told off for being “on a different planet,” or raising small whirlwinds in the garden.

vocabulary.definition: A regular daytime job with standard office hours.
vocabulary.example: The one thing Chrestomanci doesn’t have is a nine-to-five job.

vocabulary.definition: A person or thing that succeeds another.
vocabulary.example: One of the most challenging tasks a Chrestomanci has to undertake is finding his successor.

vocabulary.definition: To call upon to be present, especially as an authority or for a specific purpose.
vocabulary.example: People in dire need can also summon him by calling his name aloud three times.

vocabulary.definition: The process of assessing or evaluating something.
vocabulary.example: He is then worth testing to find out what’s stopping him from doing magic.

vocabulary.definition: To prevent someone from accomplishing something.
vocabulary.example: He has to be ready to thwart evil, apprehend villains, and solve magical conundrums.

vocabulary.definition: To test something to discover how effective or suitable it is.
vocabulary.example: Trialling it successfully for the first time on his Chrestomanci-in-training.

vocabulary.definition: Having no knowledge of a situation or fact.
vocabulary.example: They also are usually completely unaware that they have more than the usual number of lives.

vocabulary.definition: To commit oneself to and begin (an enterprise or responsibility); to take on.
vocabulary.example: One of the most challenging tasks a Chrestomanci has to undertake is finding his successor.

vocabulary.definition: A state or condition of lacking strength or a fault in someone's character or abilities.
vocabulary.example: All nine-lifed enchanters have a weakness.
